sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32400141 with Mercury Associates, Inc. to complete a right sizing study and
provide fleet consulting services. This amendment increases the contract amount by
$100,000, for a new total amount not to exceed of $225,000 for the current contract
period of December 1, 2023, through December 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding is available in FY2025 and FY2026 baseline budgets of Fund 671 Equipment
Replacement Fund in the amount of $50,000 and Fund 672 Equipment Maintenance
Fund in the amount of $50,000.
sections.current_situation:
Mercury Associates has been actively working with the Public Works’ Equipment
Maintenance Division in conducting a comprehensive fleet analysis, reviewing vehicle
replacement policies, fleet size optimization, and vehicle standardization. The consulting
services include evaluating the current processes and policies and providing
recommendations. They are also supporting the City with implementing AssetWorks
(the City’s new work order and equipment asset management software) which went live
in May 2024. Due to technical difficulties and ongoing data clean up, Staff have
continued using both FUND$ and AssetWorks. The contract amendment authorization
will ensure continued support for AssetWorks implementation, and the execution of
recommendations outlined in the Fleet Replacement Fund Short Millions Audit Report1
(Fleet Audit), including the finalization of the right sizing assessment.
Additionally, recent staffing vacancies in the Equipment Maintenance Division and the
Administrative and Fiscal Division of Public Works, have created operational
challenges. Mercury Associates are providing critical guidance on best practices to

current staff, which will help support the Departments recruitment of a new
Superintendent and Mechanics to the Equipment Maintenance Division.
Amending this contract is a Strategic Plan Priority Project, advancing our goal to provide
state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
The Department collaborated with General Services to release a Request for Proposals
- Specification No. 22-11496 on May 3, 2022, for an Equipment Maintenance/Fleet
Study as a response to the Fleet Audit. Two vendors responded, and Mercury
Associates, Inc. was selected as the most responsive and responsible vendor. While
Mercury Associates, Inc. was not the lowest bidder, their references, experience, and
staff qualifications led the evaluation committee to select them as the top submittal. The
Equipment Maintenance Division requires ongoing consulting support to improve its
overall management of staff, fleet assets, and to respond effectively to the Fleet Audit’s
recommendations.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are potential impacts to environmental sustainability and climate impacts that will
be clarified by the fleet right sizing assessment which may reduce the number of overall
fleet vehicles, which could lead to lower fuel consumption and emissions. Ensuring that
fleet reductions and replacements align with California’s Advanced Clean Fleet (ACF)
regulations, helping the City comply with upcoming zero-emission fleet requirements.
The assessment will also support the overall fleet transition away from fossil fuels to
electricity.
sections.rationale:
The City conducted a competitive procurement process and Mercury Associates was
selected the most responsive and responsible vendor. The Department has a critical
business and operational need for these consulting services, particularly due to staffing
shortages and technical challenges. Additional funding will ensure successful
implementation of the fleet audit study recommendations, improve efficiency, and
enhance fleet sustainability.
sections.alternatives:
The Department considered completing the right-sizing assessment and fleet policy
updates in-house. However, due to staffing shortages and the specialized expertise
required, this option was deemed infeasible. Contracting with an alternative vendor was
also considered, but switching consultants at this stage would cause delays and
additional costs. Extending Mercury Associates’ contract remains the most efficient and
cost-effective solution.

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32400141 AMENDMENT: MERCURY ASSOCIATES, INC. FOR
FLEET REPLACEMENT/MAINTENANCE STUDY AND CONSULTING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City released Specification No. 22-11496 on May 3, 2022 for an
Equipment Maintenance /Fleet Study; and
WHEREAS, two vendors responded, and Mercury Associates, Inc. was found to be the
most responsive and responsible bidder; and
WHEREAS, the Public Works Equipment Maintenance Division requires consulting
services to perform a comprehensive analysis of the fleet, policies and procedures related
to vehicle replacement, usage, size, and vehicle standardization.
WHEREAS, Mercury Associates, Inc. will also provide as needed fleet consulting services
including best utilization of AssetWorks; and
WHEREAS, funding for FY2025 is available in Equipment Maintenance Fund 672.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council authorizes the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract No. 32400141
with Mercury Associates, Inc. to provide fleet replacement/equipment maintenance study
and consulting services in an amount not to exceed $225,000.
